title: NHS 24 Call Handler
how_to_become: To become an NHS 24 call handler, you must complete NHS 24's core training programme. You'll then receive support as you transition into the role.

salary.entry_note: NHS 24 call handlers start at £28,011 before tax.
salary.experienced_note: Senior NHS 24 call handlers can earn up to £33,016 before tax.

description: NHS 24 provides digital and telephone-based health and care services to help people access the right care when needed. The NHS 24 Unscheduled Care Service handles 1.5 million calls annually, approximately 50% at weekends. Call handlers provide person-centred, safe, and effective healthcare on the telephone, assisting callers with minor illnesses, mental health support, or out-of-hours dental care.
role_overview: As an NHS 24 call handler, you'll accurately record information given by callers on the patient contact management system, identify their needs using system-generated guidance and additional questions, and follow instructions to determine the appropriate advice or service. Call handlers work an 8-week fixed rota. NHS 24 call handlers must join the Protecting Vulnerable Groups (PVG) scheme.

entry_requirements.overview: National 5 qualifications at SCQF level 5 or equivalent are required. Customer service or caring experience is helpful.

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying for a new job with NHSScotland.

education_and_training.training_programme: Comprehensive in-house core training programme over 3 weeks, learning to navigate the patient contact management system and connect people with vital care and support.
education_and_training.transition_support: After training, 2 weeks in a 'protected pod' supported by a manager while transitioning into the role.

learning_and_development.overview: All NHS 24 staff must complete training regularly to comply with statutory requirements. Staff are encouraged to participate in continuous professional development specific to their role.

career_progression.overview: While working at NHS 24, staff have access to learning and development opportunities to help them take on new roles and challenges.

chunks.text: To become an NHS 24 call handler, complete NHS 24's 3-week core training programme followed by 2 weeks of supported transition in a protected pod.
NHS 24 call handlers start at £28,011 before tax. Senior NHS 24 call handlers can earn up to £33,016 before tax.

NHS 24 call handlers provide person-centred healthcare on the telephone for the Unscheduled Care Service, which handles 1.5 million calls annually. They assist callers with minor illnesses, mental health support, and out-of-hours dental care.

NHS 24 call handlers accurately record caller information, identify needs using system-generated guidance, and determine appropriate advice or services. They work an 8-week fixed rota and must be registered with the PVG scheme.

National 5 qualifications at SCQF level 5 or equivalent are required. Customer service or caring experience is helpful. Useful school subjects include English, Maths, Administration and IT, and Science.
Training is delivered through a 3-week in-house core programme covering the patient contact management system, followed by 2 weeks in a protected pod with manager support.
